change: precompute-K: hoist d*x*y into the table, add_affine_point 10->8 muls, separate-K Vec layout, -kt on negated digits
note: real ~14% win, byte-identical; gain is the arithmetic; separate-K layout slightly better than inline

change: remove the per-call heap Vec in arm64 mul_index by fusing the collect+add loops
note: BIG REGRESSION: batching points into a Vec first helps locality/codegen; fusing destroyed it. one alloc << the batching benefit

change: MEASUREMENT: shared CARGO_TARGET_DIR across git worktrees
note: cargo reuses first worktree build -> baseline and candidate bench the SAME binary, deltas collapse to ~0; ALWAYS per-worktree target dirs

change: add_affine_point: strength-reduce a*=Fq::from(5) (one Montgomery mul by curve param 5) into 5a=a+4a via 3 field additions
note: FIRST fully-autonomous run (fresh agent, no staged answer). Byte-identical (differential matched), all 14 tests pass, but Delta -0.62% under the 0.99% A/A floor and bootstrap CI[-0.76,+4.16] crosses 0 -> real but sub-noise.

change: #[inline] on a per-opcode limit-tracker check (the per-opcode limit fan-out hot path)
note: BLIND vs a known perf change (answer hidden). The agent independently profiled and landed on the EXACT hot path that change optimized: the per-opcode N-dimension limit fan-out (~17% of compute). A safe #[inline] there was within-noise; the real win was the fan-out reduction (see below).

change: prompt A/B: the eliminate-redundant lens enumerated the real N-dim->1-dim fan-out reduction as top candidate but REJECTED it (unresolved invariant), chose a smaller dead-branch elimination instead
note: A/B (does iteration/lens reach the real win N-dim->1-dim?). Arm A (no lens, 2 rounds): dug deeper locally (inline -> CSE), both within-noise, NEVER considered N->1. Arm B (+ eliminate/invariant lens): GENERATED N->1 as top candidate but retreated at its safety invariant.

change: reduce a per-opcode N-dimension limit-check fan-out to a single dimension (only one dimension changes per opcode; the other dimensions self-latch at their own mutation sites)
note: WIN, the payoff of the prompt A/B. Found BLIND by the lens + adoption layer (answer hidden): it generated the N->1 candidate, RESOLVED the invariant (traced every mutator, confirmed each self-guards) instead of retreating, pinned with debug_assert + adversarial differential. Accepted -72% on the isolated kernel.

change: host::inspect_storage (REX4): eliminate the redundant middle inspect_account presence-check (3 loads/access -> 2) via a direct journal state map read; pre-REX4 path unchanged
note: REAL run on CURRENT HEAD (post-#313). Agent self-picked the fresh host-inspect lever (#313 never touched it), found a genuine ELIMINATE (same address loaded 3x per SLOAD/SSTORE; presence-check is redundant since is_created already loaded it

change: MEASUREMENT: when the judge says within-noise but the CI EXCLUDES 0 (consistent direction) and |delta|<floor, it is NOISE-LIMITED, not a non-win -- tighten the measurement, do not give up
note: Demonstrated on mega-evm host::inspect_storage: 1st judge = within-noise (delta -4.14%, floor 21.37%) because the probe per-call cost (~64ns) was so small that scheduler/frequency jitter dominated the A/A floor. Tightening ONLY measurement

change: SCOPE: ARO accepted != should-merge. The judge weighs correctness + measurable speed ONLY, not engineering cost (layering, single-responsibility, maintainability, is-a-tiny-in-memory-saving-worth-the-complexity).
note: Ground-truth from mega-evm PR #313 review (Troublor, discussion r3411840181): a SIBLING optimization -- inlining additional_limit_ext::sstore to delete a redundant warm-path inspect_storage -- was REJECTED by a human reviewer, NOT on speed

change: host::inspect_storage (REX4): elide the TAIL inspect_account too (the is_created load already hydrated code -> tail re-inspect is a no-op); compounds on the prior middle-load elision
note: COMPOUNDING round on the advanced baseline (HEAD + the prior inspect_storage middle-load elision). Agent self-picked on the advanced-baseline profile and -- crucially -- the STRUCTURAL lens (baked from the PR #313 review) WORKED: it explici
